title: antidesma subcordatum
intro: Antidesma subcordatum, also known as the Velvet Tree or Velvet-leaved Ironwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is a tropical tree native to Southeast Asia, valued for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Antidesma subcordatum can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of certain health conditions.
